enhancedCapabilities: POSIX and PowerShell installers now keep custom install and bin paths contained and do not rewrite an apexcn launcher elsewhere on PATH.
Default one-click installs still refresh a recognized older apexcn-cli launcher so existing shells resolve the new persistent installation.
Lifecycle tests now use operation-specific isolated PATH values and cannot select a developer machine launcher.
Release v0.80.5 was installed from the public latest endpoint and both local launchers now resolve the persistent package and report version 0.80.5.
unexpectedProblems: The v0.80.4 lifecycle test inherited the host PATH and rewrote /usr/local/bin/apexcn to a temporary test package that was deleted after the test, causing dist/index.js failures in later agent workflows.
The first complete post-fix test run exposed a misplaced PowerShell source assertion in the shell source test; the assertion was moved and the full suite rerun.
rootCauses: The lifecycle test supplied custom install and bin roots but did not isolate PATH, so command -v apexcn selected the real host launcher.
The installer treated default one-click installs and explicitly customized test/lifecycle installs identically when updating an already resolved launcher.
preventionActions: Only default install and bin paths may trigger shell-resolved launcher replacement; customized paths remain strictly contained.
A regression now preserves an external launcher byte-for-byte while installing successfully into custom paths.
Full test verification records the real launcher checksum before and after the suite; both hashes matched after this fix.
Every launcher-related patch release is reinstalled through the public latest one-line command and verified from both resolved and user-local launchers.
nextMilestoneGoal: Keep roadmap milestone 0.8 active and its remaining Windows lifecycle, fresh independent novice validation, and seven-day readonly soak work paused until the user explicitly resumes it.
expectedResults: Automated lifecycle qualification can no longer leave the user's apexcn command pointing at a deleted temporary directory.
Public one-click installation resolves apexcn through the persistent user installation and returns version 0.80.5.
No community API write, API Key generation, Windows lifecycle round, independent validation round, or soak run is performed by this patch.
majorRisks: Default installs intentionally retain the ability to refresh a recognized writable older launcher; ownership detection must remain narrow and covered by tests.
Real Windows lifecycle qualification remains paused, so the PowerShell change is covered by source and available local tests but is not claimed as completed Windows acceptance.
Roadmap 0.8 core acceptance criteria remain pending while the user-paused validation and soak gates are not resumed.

instructions: Re-read current repository state before planning.
Create only a just-in-time plan for the active milestone.
Resume the existing milestone; do not activate a later milestone until its acceptance and completion review are closed.
